description: System connects to Raspberry Pi over TCP on port 9000.
Sending PING must return PONG from the server.
SET <alias> <raw> sets a variable and returns OK with alias/value/unit fields.
GET <alias> reads the current variable value and returns VAL with fields.
PING must always return PONG regardless of variable state.
Raw gear value 0 must map to 0 km/h (Neutral).
Raw gear value 1 must map to 30 km/h.
Raw gear value 2 must map to 60 km/h.
Raw gear value 3 must map to 90 km/h.
Raw gear value 4 must map to 120 km/h.
Raw gear value 5 must map to 150 km/h.
Gear raw=0 is the minimum boundary value.
Gear raw=5 is the maximum boundary value.
All non-gear variables use: eng_val = scale × raw + offset clamped to [min max].
Values exceeding the max must be clamped to max.
throttle raw=0 must return 0 RPM (motor_speed min boundary).
throttle raw=1 must return 20 RPM (scale=20).
throttle raw=10 must return 200 RPM (motor_speed max boundary).
brightness raw=3 must return 30% led_brightness.
brightness raw=10 must return 100% (led_brightness max).
servo raw=5 must return 90 deg (servo_angle mid-point).
fan raw=4 must return 600 RPM (fan_speed = 150×4).
temp_set raw=1 must return 20 °C (5×1+15=20).
temp_set raw=0 must clamp to 15 °C (offset=15 is min).
led_int raw=5 must return 10 mA (led_intensity = 2×5).
pump raw=4 must return 2.0 L/min (pump_flow = 0.5×4).
voltage raw=5 must return 1.65 V (supply_voltage = 0.33×5).
pid_sp raw=6 must return 30 units (pid_setpoint = 5×6).
gain raw=3 must return 9 dB (sensor_gain = 3×3).
pressure raw=7 must return 70 kPa (pressure_setpt = 10×7).
GUI must save all test results to a CSV file with correct columns via file dialog.
test_description: Save results — verify CSV has 13 required columns

description: GUI must save the full activity log to a .txt file via file dialog.
test_description: Save log — verify .txt file has timestamped entries
